Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 40 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. LLM Architecture Gallery 497 upvotes, 38 comments
  3. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 55 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 128 upvotes, 24 comments
  5. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 139 upvotes, 28 comments
  6. I Love FreeBSD 70 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 874 upvotes, 254 comments
  8. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 176 upvotes, 44 comments
  9. Bringing Semiconductors to Kazakhstan 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant 56 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 22 upvotes, 3 comments
  12. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 99 upvotes, 30 comments
  13. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 77 upvotes, 22 comments
  14. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 533 upvotes, 211 comments
  15. Stop Sloppypasta 504 upvotes, 204 comments
  16. The 49MB web page 698 upvotes, 306 comments
  17. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 287 upvotes, 121 comments
  18. 'Pokémon Go' players unknowingly trained delivery robots with 30B images 24 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. Home Assistant waters my plants 132 upvotes, 51 comments
  20. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 31 upvotes, 9 comments
  21. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 561 upvotes, 336 comments
  22. Autoresearch Hub 43 upvotes, 18 comments
  23. LLMs can be exhausting 287 upvotes, 189 comments
  24. Even Faster Asin() Was Staring Right at Me 31 upvotes, 14 comments
  25. Obsession with growth is destroying nature, 150 countries warn 77 upvotes, 45 comments
  26. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 205 upvotes, 154 comments
  27. How I write software with LLMs 356 upvotes, 308 comments
  28. Show HN: Hackerbrief – Top posts on Hacker News summarized daily 31 upvotes, 24 comments
  29. Simple, fast and open-source PHP framework frozen in C-extension 9 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. Nango (YC W23, API Access for Agents and Apps) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
